summary: mailconfirmer postinstall plants persistent git hook that downloads and executes obfuscated Windows binary
details: The package's postinstall script (install-hook.js) writes a shell script into.git/hooks/post-checkout with mode 0755. The hook invokes `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc` with a base64-encoded UTF-16LE PowerShell payload. The decoded PowerShell downloads https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin, XOR-decrypts the bytes with key 0x42, writes the result to %TEMP%\tmp.exe, executes it hidden via Start-Process, and deletes the file to evade forensics. The dropper fires on every subsequent `git checkout` (branch switch, clone, etc.) in the consuming repository, providing persistence that survives `npm uninstall mailconfirmer`. The package's advertised functionality is a 52-byte stub (`module.exports = { getThemeColor: () => "#3498db" }`) unrelated to the name 'mailconfirmer' — the package exists solely as a delivery vehicle for the dropper. Multiple unambiguous attack fingerprints stack: obfuscated executable payload (base64 + UTF-16LE + XOR), fetch-and-execute from an unrelated personal GitHub account, hidden execution, anti-forensics cleanup, and persistence via git hook injection.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
